title: Normalization in Deep Learning
description: A comprehensive treatment of normalization techniques — from why they work to how to choose between them. Covers the covariate shift and loss landscape views, BatchNorm internals (running stats, train/eval modes, SyncBN), the LayerNorm family (RMSNorm, DeepNorm, pre/post-norm), weight and spectral normalization, small-batch alternatives (GroupNorm, InstanceNorm), and adaptive/conditional normalization (AdaIN, SPADE, FiLM, adaLN-Zero in DiT).

readings.title: Why Normalization? Covariate Shift and the Loss Landscape
readings.description: Internal covariate shift, Santurkar et al. loss landscape smoothing, β-smoothness, and a unified taxonomy of all normalization techniques across four axes

readings.title: Batch Normalization — Algorithm, Placement, and Multi-GPU
readings.description: Full BN algorithm with running statistics, the model.eval() bug, spatial BN for CNNs, pre-activation vs. post-activation placement, and Synchronized BatchNorm

readings.title: Layer Norm, RMSNorm, and the Pre-Norm / Post-Norm Debate
readings.description: LayerNorm formula and properties, pre-norm vs. post-norm stability analysis, RMSNorm's mean-centering removal and 15–20% speedup, DeepNorm's scaled residual for 1000+ layer transformers

readings.title: Weight Normalization and Spectral Normalization
readings.description: Weight norm reparameterization w = g·v/‖v‖, orthogonalized gradient updates, weight norm vs. BN comparison, spectral norm via power iteration, and WGAN Lipschitz constraint

readings.title: Group Normalization, Instance Normalization, and Spatial Variants
readings.description: Why BN degrades at small batch sizes, InstanceNorm for style transfer, GroupNorm unifying framework (GN as generalization of LN and IN), and when to use each normalization

readings.title: Adaptive and Conditional Normalization: AdaIN, SPADE, and DiT
readings.description: AdaIN style transfer via channel statistics replacement, Conditional BN / FiLM for class and language conditioning, SPADE spatially-adaptive maps for semantic image synthesis, adaLN-Zero in Diffusion Transformers

drills.title: BatchNorm, LayerNorm, Weight Norm, and Spectral Norm
drills.description: Core mechanics and failure modes of the four foundational normalization techniques

drills.title: GroupNorm, InstanceNorm, Adaptive and Conditional Normalization
drills.description: Small-batch alternatives and the progression from fixed to condition-predicted affine parameters
